anyOf.title: Some
anyOf.description: An optional value.

anyOf.title: None
anyOf.description: Nothing.

anyOf.title: Some
anyOf.description: An optional value.

anyOf.title: None
anyOf.description: Nothing.

adversarial_auditing/params/ProtocolPara.title: ProtocolParams
adversarial_auditing/params/ProtocolPara.description:
Protocol parameters for Game 1: Adversarial Auditing

 All parameters are governance-adjustable (Game 6 pathway).
 Stored in a reference UTXO at a governance-controlled address.
 Validators read params via reference inputs — no redeployment needed.

 ═══ v4 FIX: ALL TIME FIELDS IN POSIX MILLISECONDS ═══
 Plutus V3 ScriptContext uses POSIXTime in milliseconds.
 All time-related parameters MUST be in milliseconds to allow direct
 addition with submitted_at / challenged_at fields from ScriptContext.
 v3 BUG (CRITICAL-01): challenge_window was in seconds, submitted_at in ms.
 Adding them directly made a 30-minute window last 1.8 seconds.
 v4 FIX: store everything in ms. No conversion needed in validators.

 VRF research update (2026-03-23):
   SELECTION_DELAY raised from spec's 10s to 30s minimum.
   Rationale: 10s is too short; a colluding block producer
   monitoring pending challenges could grind the slot window.
   30s (~30_000 ms) meaningfully raises the attack cost.
 On-chain protocol parameters.
 CURRENT IMPLEMENTATION: baked in as a compile-time validator parameter.
 Phase 1.1 migration: move to a governance reference UTXO (see G1-ZR-13).
 Until then, changing params requires redeployment of all three validators.
 DO NOT assume reference input reading is implemented — it is not yet.

fields.title: min_claim_stake
fields.description:
Minimum AP3X stake required to submit a claim (DFM units, 6 decimals)
 Initial: 50_000_000 (50 AP3X)

fields.title: min_challenge_window
fields.description:
Minimum challenge window in POSIX milliseconds (~30 minutes)
 Initial: 1_800_000 (30 min × 60 s × 1000 ms)

fields.title: max_challenge_window
fields.description:
Maximum challenge window in POSIX milliseconds (~18 hours)
 Initial: 64_800_000 (18 h × 3600 s × 1000 ms)

fields.title: jury_size
fields.description:
Number of jurors per case (must be odd for majority)
 Initial: 5

fields.title: min_juror_bond
fields.description:
Minimum AP3X bond for jurors (DFM units, 6 decimals)
 Initial: 25_000_000 (25 AP3X)

fields.title: jury_fee_rate
fields.description:
Jury fee rate in basis points (1000 = 10% of loser's stake)
 Initial: 1000

fields.title: selection_delay
fields.description:
Delay in POSIX milliseconds before jury selection can occur.
 Prevents block producer grinding: the entropy slot is fixed after challenge.
 Initial: 30_000 (30 seconds × 1000 ms)
 Raised from spec's 10s per VRF research 2026-03-23.

fields.title: resolution_deadline
fields.description:
POSIX milliseconds after challenge for jury to resolve (~90 minutes)
 Initial: 5_400_000 (90 min × 60 s × 1000 ms)

fields.title: juror_slash_rate
fields.description:
Juror slash rate in basis points for non-participation (1000 = 10%)
 Initial: 1000

fields.title: min_agent_age
fields.description:
Minimum agent age in POSIX milliseconds (~6 hours, anti-sybil)
 Initial: 21_600_000 (6 h × 3600 s × 1000 ms)

fields.title: max_concurrent_cases
fields.description:
Maximum concurrent cases per juror
 Initial: 5

fields.title: min_jury_pool_size
fields.description:
Juror count threshold to activate jury mode (deactivate oracle)
 Initial: 10

fields.title: min_jury_pool_total
fields.description:
Minimum total juror bonds in pool for activation (DFM units)
 Initial: 250_000_000 (250 AP3X)

fields.title: oracle_active
fields.description:
Whether Foundation oracle mode is active.
 True  = Phase 1.0: Foundation resolves challenges
 False = Phase 1.1+: jury resolves challenges

fields.title: commit_window
fields.description:
Commit window in POSIX milliseconds after challenged_at.
 Jurors must submit vote commitments before challenged_at + commit_window.
 Initial: 3_600_000 (1 hour)

fields.title: reveal_window
fields.description:
Reveal window in POSIX milliseconds after commit_deadline.
 Jurors must reveal votes before challenged_at + commit_window + reveal_window.
 Initial: 1_800_000 (30 minutes)

fields.title: cleanup_buffer
fields.description:
fix: RT-V5 — Buffer in POSIX milliseconds after resolution_deadline before
 CleanupResolved is allowed. Gives jurors time to call DistributeRewards.
 Initial: 1_800_000 (30 minutes)

adversarial_auditing/types/AuditingConfi.title: AuditingConfig
adversarial_auditing/types/AuditingConfi.description:
Compile-time configuration — baked into all validators via blueprint apply.
 Contains ONLY fields that do NOT create circular deployment dependencies.
 The three cross-validator script hashes (which change after apply) are
 intentionally absent — they live in CrossValidatorRefs (a runtime UTxO datum).

fields.title: registry_policy_id
fields.description: Policy ID of the Agent Registry (for DID verification)

fields.title: ap3x_policy_id
fields.description: Policy ID of the AP3X token (the staking currency)

fields.title: ap3x_asset_name
fields.description: Asset name of the AP3X token

fields.title: oracle_vkh
fields.description: Foundation oracle verification key hash (Phase 1.0 dispute resolution)

fields.title: refs_token_policy
fields.description:
Policy ID of the NFT that authenticates the CrossValidatorRefs UTxO.
 Any reference input carrying a token under this policy is the trusted
 refs UTxO — validators parse its inline datum as CrossValidatorRefs.

 v4 FIX (CRITICAL-02): This MUST be a dedicated NativeScript policy
 locked to oracle_vkh (ScriptPubkey(oracle_vkh)), NOT the AP3X policy.
 Using AP3X policy allowed attackers to create fake refs UTxOs with
 poisoned hashes (any UTxO holding AP3X tokens would pass the check).
 The NativeScript policy ensures only the oracle can mint the refs NFT.

anyOf.title: OpenChallenge
anyOf.description: Open a new challenge against a claim (also updates claim state atomically)

anyOf.title: SubmitEvidence
anyOf.description: Submit additional evidence before jury selection

anyOf.title: OracleResolve
anyOf.description:
Phase 1.0: Foundation oracle resolves challenge with a verdict.
 Requires oracle_credential signature. No jury fee — Foundation absorbs cost.

fields.title: reasoning_hash
fields.description: blake2b_256 hash of Foundation's public reasoning (stored off-chain)

anyOf.title: ResolveJury
anyOf.description:
Phase 1.1+: Trigger jury resolution after all votes are revealed on-chain.
 Permissionless — reads revealed_verdict from juror reference inputs.

anyOf.title: TimeoutResolve
anyOf.description: Timeout: deadline passed without full jury resolution; both stakes returned.

anyOf.title: CleanupResolved
anyOf.description:
Phase 1.0+: Burn the challenge UTxO after resolution.
 Requires state == Resolved. Oracle must sign (Phase 1.0 simplicity).
 The challenge token is burned here (deferred from ResolveJury to allow
 DistributeRewards to reference the Resolved challenge UTxO).

anyOf.title: TransitionToVoting
anyOf.description:
Phase 1.1: Transition challenge from PendingJury → Voting after jury selection.
 Called atomically with jury_pool.ak SelectJury. Oracle must sign.
 selected_jurors must be a subset of eligible_jurors snapshot and match jury_size.

adversarial_auditing/types/ChallengeDatu.title: ChallengeDatum
adversarial_auditing/types/ChallengeDatu.description:
A challenge to an existing claim.
 One UTXO per challenge — references the claim UTXO being contested.

 Design note (VRF research 2026-03-23):
   `eligible_jurors` is a snapshot of juror DIDs taken at OpenChallenge time.
   This freezes the effective jury pool — prevents pool manipulation between
   OpenChallenge and SelectJury. The SelectJury tx must use this snapshot,
   not the live pool state.

fields.title: claim_ref
fields.description: Reference to the claim UTXO being challenged (cross-validator link)

fields.title: auditor_did
fields.description: DID of the challenging agent

fields.title: auditor_credential
fields.description: Payment credential of auditor (for stake return or reward)

fields.title: stake_amount
fields.description: AP3X staked by auditor (must be >= claim stake)

fields.title: evidence_hash
fields.description: blake2b_256 hash of auditor's counter-evidence (exactly 32 bytes)

fields.title: evidence_uri
fields.description: Off-chain storage URI for counter-evidence (not validated on-chain)

fields.title: challenged_at
fields.description:
POSIX time in milliseconds when challenge was opened.
 Must fall within the tx validity range (prevents backdating/future-dating).

fields.title: resolution_deadline
fields.description:
POSIX milliseconds after challenged_at for jury to resolve.
 Deadline = challenged_at + resolution_deadline.
 v4: MUST be in ms (consistent with all time fields).

fields.title: eligible_jurors
fields.description:
Snapshot of eligible juror DIDs at OpenChallenge time (sorted lexicographically).
 Freezes the pool — prevents registration/deregistration manipulation.
 Phase 1.0: may be empty list (oracle resolves without jury).
 Phase 1.1: must contain >= jury_size entries for SelectJury to proceed.

fields.title: state
fields.description: Current state of this challenge

adversarial_auditing/types/ChallengeStat.title: ChallengeState
adversarial_auditing/types/ChallengeStat.description: Lifecycle state of a challenge UTXO.

anyOf.title: PendingOracle
anyOf.description:
Awaiting Foundation oracle resolution (Phase 1.0)
 Foundation reviews evidence off-chain and submits OracleResolve tx.

anyOf.title: PendingJury
anyOf.description:
Awaiting jury selection (Phase 1.1+)
 Triggered when jury pool reaches MIN_JURY_POOL_SIZE threshold.

anyOf.title: Voting
anyOf.description: Jury selected, awaiting votes — holds the selected juror DIDs

anyOf.title: Resolved
anyOf.description: Resolved — verdict rendered, stakes distributed

anyOf.title: SubmitClaim
anyOf.description: Submit a new claim (mint handler: creates claim token + UTXO)

anyOf.title: WithdrawClaim
anyOf.description: Withdraw a validated claim after challenge window expires (no challenge)

anyOf.title: MarkChallenged
anyOf.description: Update claim state to Challenged (called atomically within OpenChallenge tx)

anyOf.title: ForfeitClaim
anyOf.description: Finalize claim after oracle/jury resolution — burn token, distribute stake

adversarial_auditing/types/ClaimDatum.title: ClaimDatum
adversarial_auditing/types/ClaimDatum.description:
A claim submitted by an agent asserting it performed some task.
 One UTXO per claim — fully parallelizable across thousands of audits.

fields.title: claimer_did
fields.description: DID of the claiming agent (policy_id of their registry NFT)

fields.title: claimer_credential
fields.description: Payment credential of claimer (for stake return)

fields.title: claim_hash
fields.description:
blake2b_256 hash of the off-chain claim data (exactly 32 bytes)
 Full claim data lives off-chain: OriginTrail UAL or IPFS CID

fields.title: claim_type
fields.description: Category tag for claim routing (e.g. "data_indexing", "task_completion")

fields.title: storage_uri
fields.description: Off-chain storage URI for full evidence (not validated on-chain)

fields.title: stake_amount
fields.description: AP3X staked by claimer (in DFM units, 6 decimals)

fields.title: submitted_at
fields.description:
POSIX time in milliseconds when claim was submitted.
 Must fall within the tx validity range (prevents backdating/future-dating).

fields.title: challenge_window
fields.description:
Challenge window in POSIX milliseconds after submission during which
 challenges are accepted. Deadline = submitted_at + challenge_window.
 Must be within [params.min_challenge_window, params.max_challenge_window].
 v4: MUST be in ms (v3 bug used seconds → 1.8s instead of 30min).

fields.title: state
fields.description: Current state of this claim

adversarial_auditing/types/ClaimState.title: ClaimState
adversarial_auditing/types/ClaimState.description: Lifecycle state of a claim UTXO.

anyOf.title: Open
anyOf.description: Open for challenges — within challenge_window

anyOf.title: Challenged
anyOf.description: A challenge has been filed — locked until resolution

anyOf.title: Validated
anyOf.description: Claim validated (no challenge, or won challenge)

anyOf.title: Invalidated
anyOf.description: Claim invalidated (lost challenge)

adversarial_auditing/types/JurorDatum.title: JurorDatum
adversarial_auditing/types/JurorDatum.description:
A juror registered in the jury pool.
 One UTXO per juror — bond (AP3X) held in UTXO value.
 `active_case`: when Some, juror cannot withdraw until case resolves.

fields.title: juror_did
fields.description: DID of the juror agent

fields.title: juror_credential
fields.description: Payment credential for reward distribution

fields.title: bond_amount
fields.description: AP3X staked as juror bond (slashed for non-participation)

fields.title: cases_resolved
fields.description: Number of challenges this juror has resolved

fields.title: majority_votes
fields.description: Number of times this juror voted with the majority

fields.title: registered_at
fields.description:
POSIX time in milliseconds when juror registered.
 v4: stored in ms (consistent with all time fields).

fields.title: active_case
fields.description:
Active case assignment: None = available, Some(token_name) = assigned to this challenge.
 Stores the challenge token name (ByteArray), which is immutable across the challenge
 lifecycle (derived at OpenChallenge mint, survives all spends including ResolveJury).
 fix: CR-002-F1 — changed from Option<OutputReference> to Option<ByteArray>
 because OutputReference mutates on every spend, breaking cross-validator lookups
 after ResolveJury consumes and re-outputs the challenge UTxO.

fields.title: vote_commitment
fields.description:
Commit-reveal voting: blake2b_256 hash of (verdict_index_byte || salt).
 Set during CommitVote, cleared during RevealVote.
 None = no commitment yet (or already revealed).

fields.title: revealed_verdict
fields.description:
Commit-reveal voting: revealed verdict after RevealVote.
 None = not yet revealed. Some(verdict) = juror has revealed their vote.

anyOf.title: RegisterJuror
anyOf.description: Register as a juror — stake bond, mint juror token

anyOf.title: SelectJury
anyOf.description:
Phase 1.0: Foundation oracle selects jury.
   - oracle_credential signs the tx
   - selection_seed is cosmetic (used for ordering only; validator does NOT
     trust it for security — oracle controls selection in Phase 1.0)

 Phase 1.1: replace oracle-signed selection with slot-based entropy
 once pool is large enough (see VRF research 2026-03-23).
 // Phase 1.1: replace with VRF-based selection once research complete

fields.title: challenge_ref
fields.description: OutputReference of the challenge UTXO this jury serves

fields.title: selection_seed
fields.description: Cosmetic ordering seed (Phase 1.0: oracle-provided, not security-critical)

fields.title: selected_jurors
fields.description:
Pre-computed list of selected juror DIDs
 Phase 1.0: oracle picks; validator checks oracle sig only
 Phase 1.1: validator re-derives from slot entropy and verifies

anyOf.title: CommitVote
anyOf.description:
Commit a vote hash on an active challenge (Phase 1.1 commit-reveal)
 Replaces CastVote — juror submits blake2b_256(verdict_index_byte || salt)

fields.title: commitment_hash
fields.description: blake2b_256(verdict_index_byte || salt) — 32 bytes

anyOf.title: RevealVote
anyOf.description: Reveal a previously committed vote (Phase 1.1 commit-reveal)

anyOf.title: DistributeRewards
anyOf.description: Distribute rewards and update stats after challenge resolution

anyOf.title: WithdrawJuror
anyOf.description: Withdraw juror registration (only if active_case == None)

anyOf.title: ReceiveJuryFee
anyOf.description:
fix: NEW-FINAL-02 — accept jury fee UTXOs at jury_pool_hash.
 A ResolveJury tx routes the jury fee to jury_pool_hash with a FeeAccumulator datum.
 This action allows that UTXO to be consumed later (e.g., via DistributeRewards
 or a future FeeDistribute action in Phase 1.1).
 Requires oracle signature to prevent unauthorized drainage.

anyOf.title: SlashNonReveal
anyOf.description:
Slash a juror who committed but did not reveal within the reveal window.
 Oracle-gated to prevent griefing. Bond reduced by slash_rate.

anyOf.title: ClaimerWins
anyOf.description: Claimer was correct — auditor loses stake

anyOf.title: AuditorWins
anyOf.description: Auditor was correct — claimer loses stake

anyOf.title: Inconclusive
anyOf.description: Inconclusive — both stakes returned minus jury fee

cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output
